message:
fix(plan): use auto-advance runtime and timed sleep for deterministic plan execution

SharedLabHandle::wait_for_quiescent now uses crate::time::sleep with a
10ms duration instead of lab_yield_once. The bare yield was insufficient
when the runtime needed to advance its virtual clock to unblock timed
futures — it would spin indefinitely without making progress because
no time elapsed between yields.

execute_plan_in_lab_core now calls runtime.run_with_auto_advance()
instead of runtime.run_until_quiescent() for both the initial run and
the convergence loop iterations. run_with_auto_advance drives the
virtual clock forward automatically, allowing timed futures (sleeps,
timeouts, deadlines) to fire without external clock manipulation. This
prevents the plan executor from hanging when the plan contains steps
that depend on wall-clock progress, such as retry delays or heartbeat
timeouts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
